title: Opinion: Why Energy Companies Are Tomorrow's Dividend Stocks To Retire On
text: Dividend stocks are a popular choice for retirement and REITs and MLPs have been the preferred high-dividend payers of the past and currently. There is a new and potentially better way to retire with dividends that will become popular with time: Energy Exploration and Production Companies.

title: Why ConocoPhillips Is A Buy For The Right Investor
text: Conoco Phillips is a globally diversified company worth a closer look due to its positions in oil plays like Eagle Ford, Bakken, and the Permian Regions. Owning these companies today could be similar to owning them in 2002, with potential for long-term growth as hydrocarbon prices begin to rise. ConocoPhillips has attractive valuation metrics and its positions in the Delaware and Midland Basins make it a promising investment opportunity with an attractive dividend.

title: ConocoPhillips: Steady, Simply Steady
text: ConocoPhillips sees pressure on current earnings due to lower price realizations and inflationary pressures. Net capital spending in 2023 was only set to stabilize production, but is now sufficient to grow production again. A recent rally in energy prices may halt earnings declines, as Conoco is well-positioned to grow production and reward investors from here onward.

title: Keep a Close Watch on These 3 Bakken Stocks: Here's Why
text: Favorable oil price is backing modestly higher production volumes in Bakken. Companies that are well-poised to gain are Hess (HES), Marathon Oil (MRO) and ConocoPhillips (COP).

title: Here's Why Hold Strategy is Apt for ConocoPhillips (COP) Now
text: Favorable oil price is aiding ConocoPhillips' (COP) bottom line. However, increasing production and operating expenses are hurting it.

title: ConocoPhillips: A Value-Rich Energy Operator
text: ConocoPhillips has a strategic 10-year plan focused on restrained investment, steady growth and returning cash to shareholders. The company is diversifying its energy assets intelligently. ConocoPhillips is surprisingly nimble given its conservative approach. The strategic plan drives coherent execution and opportunistic tactical moves.

title: Top Wall Street analysts pick these dividend stocks for solid returns
text: TipRanks analyst ranking service pinpoints Wall Street's best-performing stocks, including McDonald's and Chevron.

title: Ex-Div Reminder For ConocoPhillips
text: On 8/15/23, ConocoPhillips will trade ex-dividend, for its quarterly dividend of $0.51, payable on 9/1/23.

title: ConocoPhillips (COP) to Sell Notes for Financing Surmount Buyout
text: ConocoPhillips (COP) is set to offer notes in order to raise money for its remaining 50% stake acquisition in Surmount plant.

title: ConocoPhillips to offer notes to fund $3 bln Surmont oil facility buyout
text: ConocoPhillips will offer notes to raise funds for its acquisition of the remaining 50% stake in Surmont oil facility in Canada worth $3 billion, the U.S. oil gas producer said in a filing on Tuesday.

title: ConocoPhillips signs 20-year LNG supply deals with Mexico Pacific
text: ConocoPhillips has signed 20-year deals to receive a collective 2.2 million tons of liquefied natural gas (LNG) a year from Mexico Pacific's Saguaro export facility, the U.S. oil and gas producer said on Thursday.

title: Wall Street Lunch: Ackman Vs. Treasuries
text: Bill Ackman bets against 30-year U.S. Treasuries; Q2 nonfarm labor productivity up 3.7%. COP misses on Q2 adj EPS, QCOM tumbles on mixed Q3 report; Tesla sold 64.3k China-made EVs in July.

title: ConocoPhillips (COP) Lags on Q2 Earnings, Ups '23 Output View
text: Lower average realized oil equivalent prices hurt ConocoPhillips' (COP) earnings in Q2.

title: ConocoPhillips (COP) Reports Q2 Earnings: What Key Metrics Have to Say
text: While the top- and bottom-line numbers for ConocoPhillips (COP) give a sense of how the business performed in the quarter ended June 2023, it could be worth looking at how some of its key metrics compare to Wall Street estimates and year-ago values.

title: ConocoPhillips Misses Earnings Expectations Despite Record Production
text: Energy giant ConocoPhillips posted second-quarter profit and revenue that missed expectations, reflecting a pullback in oil prices from last year's highs and despite record production levels.

title: ConocoPhillips (COP) Q2 Earnings and Revenues Lag Estimates
text: ConocoPhillips (COP) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.84 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.94 per share. This compares to earnings of $3.91 per share a year ago.

title: ConocoPhillips stock slips as lower crude price comparatives bite quarterly earnings
text: ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) stock traded down in Thursday's premarket deals as it confirmed a steep decline in financial metrics for the second quarter, reflecting softer crude oil pricing compared to last year's peaks. The Houston-headquartered oiler reported US$2.2 billion of earnings (adjusted EBITDA), US$1.84 per share, from US$5.1 billion and US$3.96 a year ago.

title: ConocoPhillips stock falls after profit, revenue miss expectations as realized prices tumbled 38%
text: Shares of ConocoPhillips COP, -1.52% fell 1.4% in premarket trading Thursday, after the oil exploration and production company reported second-quarter profit and revenue that missed expectations as prices tumbled, while production reached record levels and the full-year outlook was raised. Net income fell to $2.23 billion, or $1.84 a share, from $5.15 billion, or $3.96 a share, in the year-ago period.

title: ConocoPhillips joins shale peers in reporting Q2 profit slump
text: U.S. shale producer ConocoPhillips reported a fall in second-quarter profit on Thursday, as oil and gas prices dropped from last year's peaks.

title: ConocoPhillips Reports Second-Quarter 2023 Results; Raises Full-Year Production Guidance and Declares Quarterly Dividend and Variable Return of Cash Distribution
text: HOUSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--ConocoPhillips (NYSE: COP) today reported second-quarter 2023 earnings and adjusted earnings of $2.2 billion, or $1.84 per share.

title: ConocoPhillips Q2 Earnings: What Are We Watching?
text: We expect ConocoPhillips stock to trade lower post Q2 with both revenue and earnings missing market expectations.

title: The Top 3 Stocks to Buy TOMORROW
text: If you're a big believer in Warren Buffett's slow-and-steady approach to the capital market, you're not going to find much value in these stocks to buy tomorrow. Frankly, this list centers almost exclusively on hubris; that is, the audacity to assume how the market will respond on any given day.

title: Earnings Previews: AB-InBev, ConocoPhillips, Warner Bros Discovery
text: After U.S. markets closed on Monday, Diamondback Energy reported revenue that beat Wall Street estimates by 1.2% and fell more than 30% year over year.
